location.office_time_required: Ottawa or Toronto preferred; proximity to government customers is important

level_signals: Eligible to obtain Top Secret Canadian government security clearance
Direct engagement with departmental CIOs and senior government officials

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time in enterprise SaaS, infrastructure, or platform products sold into regulated industries.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time in enterprise SaaS, infrastructure, or platform products sold into regulated industries

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct experience working with or within Canadian government, defence, or public sector organizations, whether as a vendor, contractor, or public servant.
Strong cross-functional collaborator who can align engineering, sales, legal, and customer teams around a shared plan without formal authority.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to present to senior government officials and translate technical concepts for non-technical decision-makers.
Eligible to obtain Top Secret Canadian government security clearance.
credentials_named: Top Secret Canadian government security clearance

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Active Canadian government security clearance (Secret or Top Secret). Strong preference for candidates holding active Top Secret clearance, as this enables immediate engagement with defence and national security accounts.

Strong working knowledge of the Canadian government technology landscape: data sovereignty requirements, Treasury Board policies, security classification frameworks (Protected B/C, Secret), and procurement processes.
Experience with GC Cloud services (Azure Government, AWS GovCloud) or Protected B/C deployment environments.

scope.surface: North agentic AI platform — public sector and defence vertical (Canadian government accounts)
scope.business_model: enterprise B2G (business-to-government), SaaS platform sold into regulated Canadian public sector
primary_metrics_inferred: Compliance milestone achievement (Protected B/C, Top Secret environments)
Deal support and RFP win rate
Customer adoption and expansion within government accounts
core_responsibilities: Own the public sector product roadmap for North, prioritizing features and compliance investments based on direct customer input, deal requirements, and the regulatory landscape.

Translate government requirements, including data sovereignty, Protected B/C environments, Privacy Act compliance, Algorithmic Impact Assessments, accessibility standards, and official languages obligations, into actionable product requirements for internal engineering and design teams.
Work directly with our FDEs and Applied Machine Learning Engineers to scope and build custom solutions/agents/features for public sector accounts.
Partner with GTM and EPM teams to support RFP responses, technical evaluations, and procurement processes, ensuring Cohere's product capabilities are accurately and compellingly represented.
Build and maintain deep customer relationships with departmental CIOs, IT directors, and technical leads, acting as a trusted advisor on AI adoption in constrained regulatory environments.

Synthesize market and customer signals from across the public sector vertical into a coherent product strategy, including competitive positioning against other AI platforms pursuing government contracts.

green_flags: Clearly scoped vertical ownership (Canadian public sector and defence) with well-defined customer base
Role sits at a strategic intersection of product, compliance, and vertical strategy — high-impact
Explicit acknowledgement that candidates need not meet every qualification ('we still encourage you to apply')
Generous perks: 6 weeks vacation, full health/dental, 100% parental leave top-up for 6 months, personal enrichment benefits
Hybrid model with flexibility — offices in multiple cities and co-working stipend
Company mission aligned to frontier AI with strong research and engineering culture
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed at all
Eligibility to obtain Top Secret clearance is a hard requirement — limits candidate pool significantly
Strong preference for active Top Secret clearance in nice-to-haves effectively raises the bar further
Ottawa or Toronto preferred — not fully remote, may eliminate strong candidates outside those cities
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD — unclear for non-Canadian candidates
Bilingual (English/French) listed as nice-to-have but may be effectively required for some government accounts

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from '7+ years' requirement, 'senior' descriptor, and scope of role)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false — no mention of direct reports)
scope.business_model (inferred as B2G from public sector focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: Location listed as 'Ottawa or Toronto preferred' with 'hybrid work model' mentioned in perks — it is unclear whether fully remote (from elsewhere in Canada) is an option or if physical proximity to Ottawa/Toronto is a firm expectation.

Security clearance eligibility (hard requirement) vs. active clearance (nice-to-have) creates a two-tier expectation; 'strong preference' language for active Top Secret effectively elevates this soft requirement close to hard.

No mention of Canadian citizenship or work authorization requirements despite the role requiring Canadian government security clearance eligibility, which typically requires Canadian citizenship or permanent residency.
